a.title: Sync with CSMs around AFun/IKU Requirements + Deadlines
Sync with Netzer before Wednesday HollywoodBets meeting
a.description: Before the Wednesday meeting with Neil and Wade from HollywoodBets, sync with Netzer to align on what LTS capabilities exist vs are planned, and agree on messaging for client questions about current vs future features.
a.meetingContext: Harrison and Ziv agreed they need to present LTS capabilities carefully — the Defend product is still in development and they need to agree on what to demo vs what to position as upcoming. Ziv noted the Wednesday meeting involves Neil and Wade.
suggestedApproach: Message Netzer today — set up a 30 min call before Wednesday
Agree on: what we can demo live, what we describe as 'in build', what we avoid mentioning
Also pull in Anders if the prediction markets call gives useful context on MTS capabilities

a.title: Check if Hollywoodbets have shared LTS scope ahead of meeting
a.description: On the Wednesday call with HollywoodBets (Neil and Wade), Harrison to lead with discovery questions to understand exactly what services and risk management responsibilities they expect LTS to provide, rather than pitching features.
a.meetingContext: HollywoodBets currently uses Radar exclusively but wants to add LSports as a secondary provider for additional content and markets, to compete with Betway. They are a large operator — 4,000+ employees. Key is understanding their specific LTS requirements before positioning.
suggestedApproach: Open with: 'Before we walk you through LTS, can you help us understand what you're looking for specifically?'
Key questions: What does your current risk management setup look like? What gaps does Radar leave? Are you looking for us to manage sports feeds, trade configuration, both?
Take notes on their answers — use these to tailor the Defend/LTS positioning

a.title: Sync with Anders to understand MTS capabilities vs LTS
a.description: After the upcoming prediction markets call, sync with Anders to understand what MTS currently offers and how it compares to LTS, so Harrison can better prepare for the HollywoodBets meeting and position LTS accurately.
a.meetingContext: Harrison: Sync with Anders after the upcoming prediction markets call to understand MTS capabilities and compare them to LTS, to better prepare for the Hollywood Bets meeting.
suggestedApproach: Attend / check in after the prediction markets call
Ask Anders: what does MTS currently offer that LTS doesn't, and vice versa
Use the comparison to sharpen the HollywoodBets pitch

a.title: Follow up with Netzer re: Playtech outsourcing for LATAM customer support
a.description: Harrison to follow up with Nessa about reaching out to Playtech's outsourced trading operation for potential customer support coverage in Latin America.
a.meetingContext: Yoav and Harrison discussed outsourcing options for LATAM support, given the language requirements (Spanish helpful but not mandatory). Playtech's outsourced operation came up as a potential solution.
suggestedApproach: Message Nessa — ask if she's made contact with Playtech's outsourced team yet
If not, get the company name and contact (Ashkelon was going to send this earlier)
Assess whether they could cover LATAM-hours customer support for managed services clients

a.title: Review Benel's competition tiering PRD and incorporate into LTS/Defend requirements
a.description: Gavin committed to touching base with Benel to get the PRD for his competition tiering work, and incorporate the relevant output into the LTS/Defend requirements. Harrison needs to stay closely aligned on this as it feeds directly into the Defend product scope.
a.meetingContext: Gavin: 'I'll touch base with Benel now, and get the PRD, and I'll add it into something that we want to do — stuff that we look to consume their output.'
suggestedApproach: Follow up with Gavin to confirm he has the PRD and ask for a copy
Review Benel's tiering design against the current LTS/Defend competition tiering requirements
Identify gaps or alignment points and note what can be consumed vs what needs to be built differently
a.dependencies: Gavin getting PRD from Benel first

a.title: Prepare cash-out brief and 1-3 targeted customer questions for Daniel
a.description: Daniel requested a brief description of what cash-out is, plus 1-3 targeted questions about cash-out usage for him to share with customers to investigate market needs. Harrison noted cash-out would be the third biggest product if measured as a sport at Flutter — a significant opportunity worth investigating.
a.meetingContext: Daniel: 'Prepare a brief description of what cash-out is, along with 1-3 targeted questions about cash-out usage, for Daniel to share with customers and investigate market needs.'
suggestedApproach: Write a 3-5 sentence plain-English description of cash-out (what it is, why it matters for B2B clients, the bet builder angle)
Draft 3 customer questions: e.g. 'Do you currently offer cash-out?' / 'What % of your GGR comes from cash-out?' / 'What's your biggest pain point with current cash-out providers?'
Send to Daniel via Slack or email — keep it brief, this is an input for his customer conversations

a.title: Meet with Valerie (Chief of Staff) as part of onboarding
a.description: As part of the standard onboarding process, Harrison needs to meet with Valerie, LSports' Chief of Staff.
a.meetingContext: Next steps: 'Harrison: Meet with Valerie (Chief of Staff) as part of onboarding and team introductions.'
suggestedApproach: Send Valerie a short intro message and request a 20-30 minute intro call
Come with a brief on your role and what you're working on so the conversation is productive
Ask about any admin/operational things she handles that you should know about

a.title: Target 90% automation of risk management processes within 2 months
a.description: Dotan asked Harrison to work toward automating risk management processes to handle all bets in an automated way — targeting 90% automation with 10% manual review — within approximately 2 months. This is a strategic goal, not a single task, but Harrison needs to drive it.
a.meetingContext: Next steps: 'Harrison: Automate risk management processes to handle all bets in an automated way, aiming for 90% automation with 10% manual review, within the next 2 months.'
suggestedApproach: Define what 'automated' means for each bet type — pre-match, in-play, bet builder — and identify which decisions are already rule-based vs require judgement
Map the current manual touchpoints in the risk process and prioritise the highest-volume ones for automation first
Draft an automation roadmap and share with Itsik and Daniel for sign-off
a.dependencies: Input from trading/risk team on current process

a.title: Work with Claude/AI to backtest risk patterns and feed expertise into PRD
a.description: Dotan asked Harrison to use Claude/AI tools to backtest and identify risk patterns, and to feed his own domain expertise and pain points into a PRD for the development team. This links closely to the automation goal.
a.meetingContext: Next steps: 'Harrison: Work with Claude/AI to backtest and identify risk patterns, and feed expertise and pain points into PRD for development.'
suggestedApproach: Start by documenting 5-10 specific risk scenarios from your Flutter experience where automated rules worked well or failed
Use Claude to help structure these into backtestable hypotheses
Create a risk PRD section covering: bet types, trigger conditions, automated actions, and escalation rules

a.title: Pass Yoav & Sales teams the internal LTS product timelines
a.description: Dotan asked Harrison to catch up with Yoav to share internal numbers and timelines, and to coordinate pushing back the onboarding of strategically important clients by ~3 months to ensure operational readiness before they go live.
a.meetingContext: Next steps: 'Harrison: Catch up with Yoav next week to provide internal numbers and timelines, and coordinate pushing back onboarding of strategically important clients for 3 months to ensure operational readiness.'
suggestedApproach: Prepare a brief update on LTS readiness status, timeline to MVP, and known gaps
Agree with Yoav on which clients are 'strategically important' and which can proceed now
Document the agreed delay and communicate to relevant stakeholders

a.title: Include bet profiling model milestones in the client-facing LTS roadmap
a.description: Peter's model development timeline needs to be visible in the LTS roadmap that clients and sales see — including the 24/7 ops readiness target by September 1st.
a.meetingContext: Harrison to include bet profiling model development and testing milestones in the upcoming public/client-facing roadmap, ensuring readiness targets (e.g., 24/7 operations by September 1st) are clearly communicated.
suggestedApproach: Add Peter's model phases to the LTS timeline: data matching complete, retrospective scoring, prospective testing, live deployment
Align with Peter on realistic dates first
Incorporate into the roadmap doc being prepared for management
a.meetingTitle: Bet Profiling Catch Up w/ Peter

a.title: Check with Itsik about attending the Lisbon trade show
a.description: Harrison to confirm with Itsik whether he should attend the Lisbon trade show, and if approved, coordinate with Yifat for travel arrangements and introductions to the relevant people.
a.meetingContext: Harrison to check with ITSIC about attending the Lisbon trade show and, if approved, coordinate with Yifat to get connected with the relevant people for travel arrangements.
suggestedApproach: Message Itsik: 'Is it worth me attending Lisbon? What's the format and who's going?'
If yes — connect with Yifat to sort travel and introductions
If no — no further action needed

a.title: Catch up with Adi Schwartz to review the player segmentation model
a.description: Mor asked Harrison to follow up with Adi Schwartz to review the player segmentation model and understand how it could feed into LTS risk decisions.
a.meetingContext: Harrison to catch up with Adi Schwartz to review the player segmentation model.
suggestedApproach: Message Adi to set up a 30-min call or walkthrough
Review the model features list Mor is sending
Come out with a clear view on how segmentation output can be used in Defend's risk rules
a.dependencies: Mor sending the features list and model docs
a.meetingTitle: Data Science / ML Catch Up w/ Mor

a.title: Discuss AI/ML Model DEFEND integration with Gavin/Maayan
a.description: Harrison to schedule a catch-up with Mayan once she returns from maternity leave to discuss how the ML models (player segmentation, bet profiling) integrate with the Defend product.
a.meetingContext: Harrison to catch up with Mayan (after her return from maternity leave) to discuss integration of models with Defend product.
suggestedApproach: Note in your calendar to reach out when Mayan returns
Prepare a brief on the model integration questions you want to cover
Schedule a 45-min session
a.meetingTitle: Data Science / ML Catch Up w/ Mor

a.title: Map out an operational coverage plan (including night shifts) for Itsik and Netzer
a.description: Harrison to plan out how operational coverage will work — including who handles nights and weekends — and present this to Itsik and Netzer for feedback and approval.
a.meetingContext: Harrison to map out a plan for operational coverage (including potential use of support team members for night shifts) and present to Itsik and Netzer for feedback and approval.
suggestedApproach: Define the coverage model: 24/7 from day 1, or phased?
Map the Poland support team option for nights
Show hourly coverage gaps and proposed solutions
Link to the overnight support plan being built for Netta/Yalo
a.meetingTitle: Job Descriptions / Staffing Catch Up w/ Itsik

c.title: Look into Playtech's outsourced trading company in Bulgaria
c.description: Raised as a potential source of trading talent or outsourced support. Harrison to investigate what Playtech's Bulgarian operation does and whether it's relevant for LTS staffing.
c.meetingContext: Harrison to look into Playtech's outsourced company in Bulgaria for potential trading support.
suggestedApproach: Ask Ashkelon for the company name (they committed to send it)
Do a quick search to understand their scale, capabilities, and cost model
Assess fit for LTS overnight trading support

c.title: Prepare clear messaging for clients on platform/market/competition limits
c.description: Harrison to prepare clear client-facing messaging on how platform limits, market limits, and competition limits work — especially for low-liquidity or custom markets. Needed for IKU and upcoming onboardings.
c.meetingContext: Harrison to prepare clear messaging/expectations for clients regarding platform/market/competition limits and liability management, especially for low-liquidity or custom markets.
suggestedApproach: Draft a 1-page client explainer on the limits hierarchy (platform > market > competition)
Include guidance on what clients can configure vs what LSports sets centrally
Review with Gavin before sending to any clients

c.title: Review the cash-out market landscape — providers, usage, customer satisfaction
c.description: Daniel asked Harrison to conduct a review of the cash-out market: who provides it, how customers use it, satisfaction levels, and what competitors are doing. Given Harrison's Flutter background, this should be achievable from memory + light research.
c.meetingContext: Next steps: 'Harrison: Conduct a review of the cash-out market/landscape (who provides cash-out, usage by customers, satisfaction, competitors).'
suggestedApproach: Draw on Flutter/SkyBet experience to outline the major cash-out providers (Kambi, SBTech, OpenBet, etc.) and their positioning
Note key customer satisfaction pain points from your experience (margin, speed, accuracy, bet builder cash-out limitations)
Package as a 1-page brief or slide — can be used to feed into a future PRD or sales material

c.title: Coordinate with Shira to get bet/customer data ingested into Databricks for reporting
c.description: Harrison (and Gavin) to work with Shira's BI team to get bet and customer data flowing into Databricks, enabling reporting and liability analysis. This was flagged in both the Defend scope meeting and the LTS Sync.
c.meetingContext: Gavin (and/or Harrison): Ensure bet and customer data is ingested into Databricks for reporting and analysis (coordinate with Ash Shearer if needed).
suggestedApproach: Speak with Gavin first to define the exact data requirements (which fields, what granularity)
Connect Shira and Gavin so the BI team can scope the work
Confirm timeline — Shira needs this to move forward on reporting

c.title: Provide clear timelines to the sales team on when the managed trading service will be ready
c.description: The sales team needs to know when they can start onboarding different types of clients. Harrison to produce and communicate a clear readiness timeline — e.g. 'basic clients by X, full-service by Y'.
c.meetingContext: Harrison to provide clear timelines to the sales team regarding when the new risk/managed trading service will be ready for onboarding different types of clients (e.g., by September 1st for certain client types).
suggestedApproach: Define 2-3 client tiers by complexity and what 'ready to onboard' means for each
Agree the dates internally first (with Itsik)
Send a short brief to the sales team — Yifat, Tina, Laurie, Yalo — so they can set correct expectations
c.meetingTitle: Data Integrity Catch Up w/ Aviv

c.title: Contact GTM (start with Tom Randler) for LTS opportunity list by region and revenue
c.description: Harrison to reach out to the GTM team to get a list of potential LTS opportunities, split by region and projected revenue — to feed into the business case and staffing plan.
c.meetingContext: Harrison to contact GTM department (starting with Tom Randler) to get list of potential LTS opportunities split by region and projected revenue.
suggestedApproach: Message Tom Randler — brief intro to the ask, what format you want the data in, and why
Follow up with a meeting invite to go through the list together
Use the output to build the client scenarios and staffing plan

c.title: Lead discovery on Wednesday's HollywoodBets call — clarify exactly what they need from LTS
c.description: On the Wednesday call with HollywoodBets (Neil and Wade), Harrison to lead with discovery questions rather than a product demo. The goal is to understand exactly what risk management responsibilities they want LTS to own, so LSports can scope a realistic offer. HollywoodBets are a large SA sportsbook (4,000+ employees), currently using Radar exclusively, wanting LTS as a secondary provider for additional content/markets.
c.meetingContext: Harrison: Ask Hollywood Bets representatives on the Wednesday call to clarify exactly what services and risk management responsibilities they expect LTS to provide.
suggestedApproach: Open with: 'Before we show you anything, can you walk us through exactly what you'd want LTS to handle vs what stays with Radar?'
Key areas to probe: which sports/markets, do they want full risk ops or just feed + config, what does their current Radar setup look like
Position LTS operational readiness (Sep 1 target) as a feature, not a limitation

c.title: Sync with Netzer (and possibly Anders) before Wednesday's HollywoodBets meeting
c.description: Before the Wednesday call with HollywoodBets (Neil and Wade), sync with Netzer to align on what LTS capabilities actually exist vs are planned, and agree on how to message the gap honestly without losing the deal. HollywoodBets want LTS as a secondary risk provider alongside Radar for South African operations, e-cricket and e-soccer.
c.meetingContext: Harrison: Sync with Netzel (and possibly Anders) before the Wednesday meeting to align on what LTS capabilities exist or are planned, and agree on messaging for client questions about current vs. future features.
suggestedApproach: Message Netzer today — flag the Wednesday HollywoodBets call and ask for 30 mins to align on messaging
Key questions: what can we demo now (bet monitor, risk controls), what's the honest timeline for Defend, how do we position vs Radar
Agree on what to promise vs what to present as 'in development'

c.title: Review and tweak the initial GTX onboarding questionnaire, create Google Form version
c.description: Harrison was asked to review and potentially adjust the initial GTX questionnaire used for customer onboarding, and create a Google Form version of it for easier distribution and data capture.
c.meetingContext: Next steps: 'Harrison: Review and potentially tweak the initial GTX questionnaire and create a Google form version for customer onboarding.'
suggestedApproach: Get a copy of the current GTX questionnaire from Daniel or Itsik
Review for completeness and relevance to the LTS onboarding process
Recreate as a Google Form and share the link with Daniel
c.dependencies: Getting the existing questionnaire from Daniel

c.title: Define the operational process for overnight/ops support within 3-5 weeks
c.description: Yalo needs clarity on what the overnight operations support process will look like so they can plan customer commitments and involve the Poland team if needed.
c.meetingContext: Harrison to work out in the next 3-5 weeks what the operational process might look like for overnight/operations support, and then coordinate with Yalo to determine required support.
suggestedApproach: Map the hours where manual oversight is needed vs automated rules can handle it
Define escalation path for overnight issues
Agree with Yalo what Poland team handles vs what needs a trading expert

c.title: Propose an overnight support plan (potentially using Poland team) to Netta
c.description: Harrison to consider options for overnight coverage and propose a plan to Netta — possibly leveraging the Poland support team for night shifts with clear operational instructions.
c.meetingContext: Harrison to consider and propose to Netta a plan for overnight support, potentially leveraging the Poland team, and define clear operational instructions for them.
suggestedApproach: Define what 'overnight support' actually needs to cover for managed trading clients
Assess Poland team capacity and willingness
Draft a simple operational runbook (escalation steps, what decisions they can make vs escalate)
Present to Netta as a proposal

c.title: Build a Claude Co-pilot Skill for L Sports Defend as a proof of concept
c.description: Daniel proposed creating a 'L Sports Defend' Claude skill in Cowork — e.g. 'Defend trade' or 'defense analysis' — as a learning exercise and proof of concept for how AI agents could automate tasks within Defend. Harrison expressed enthusiasm and agreed to pursue this.
c.meetingContext: Daniel: 'Build a Claude Co-pilot Skill for L Sports Defend (e.g. Defend trade or defense analysis) as a proof of concept/learning exercise.'
suggestedApproach: Start with a simple skill — e.g. one that reads a SKILL.md and can configure a Defend setting or answer questions about a customer's risk setup
Use the existing Defend skill you already have in /mnt/skills/user/defend-settings/ as a foundation
Demo to Daniel when working — even a 10-minute demo of a basic task will validate the concept

c.title: Sync with Anders after prediction markets call — understand MTS capabilities vs LTS
c.description: After today's prediction markets ecosystem call, sync with Anders to understand MTS capabilities and compare them to LTS. Use this to better prepare for the HollywoodBets meeting.
c.meetingContext: Ziv suggested pulling in Anders to understand MTS capabilities before the HollywoodBets meeting — knowing the MTS feature set will help Harrison position LTS correctly and avoid overlap or contradiction.
suggestedApproach: Catch Anders at the end of or after the prediction markets call
Ask: what does MTS currently handle vs what LTS is expected to own?
Use the answer to refine the HollywoodBets messaging

c.title: Chat with Netzer about Claude-based reporting/visualisation tools
c.description: Discuss the feasibility and approach for building reporting/visualisation tools using Claude, covering both fake/test data and production data scenarios. Harrison volunteered this himself during the meeting. Key questions: how naive is it to build something with fake data in Claude? Can we build live production visualisations? The conclusion from the meeting was that basic CSV-import tools are definitely feasible; live data is the open question.
c.meetingContext: Harrison: 'I'll chat to Netza as well, just be like, how naive is it to think that we can build something with fake data in the back of Claude... can we build some of this stuff in production using Claude?'
suggestedApproach: Message Netzer to schedule a short call or async chat this week
Prepare 2-3 specific examples of what you'd want to build (e.g. CSV import visualiser, live bet monitoring dashboard) to ground the conversation
Come out with a clear yes/no on production data feasibility and a rough timeline
